{% extends "base.html" %} {% load i18n %} {% block title %}{% translate "Setup Guide" %}{% endblock %} {% block breadcrumb %}
  • {% translate "Documentation" %}
  • {% translate "Setup" %}
    • {% endblock %} {% block content %}
    {% translate "On This Page" %}
    • {% translate "Docker (Recommended)" %}
    • {% translate "Manual Setup" %}
    • {% translate "Next Steps" %}

    {% translate "Setup Guide" %}

    {% translate "Get your development environment ready in minutes." %}

    {% translate "Development Only:" %} {% translate "This setup is for local development. See" %} {% translate "Deployment Guide" %} {% translate "for production." %}

    {% translate "Docker (Recommended)" %}

    {% translate "Docker handles all dependencies and keeps your system clean. Perfect for local development." %}

    {% translate "1. Create Your Repository" %}

    {% translate "Click the" %} {% translate "Use this template" %} {% translate "button on" %} {% translate "GitHub" %} {% translate "to create your own repository." %}

    {% translate "2. Clone Your Repo" %}

    git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/YOUR_PROJECT.git
cd YOUR_PROJECT

    {% translate "3. Install Docker" %}

    {% translate "Docker Desktop" %} {% translate "(or any Docker runtime)" %}

    {% translate "For nerds:" %} Lazydocker {% translate "- terminal UI for Docker (optional but awesome)" %}

    {% translate "4. Create .env file" %}

    cp .env.example .env

    {% translate "5. Edit .env" %}

    {% translate "Edit the .env file with your project details. The required fields are:" %}

    • PROJECT_SLUG - {% translate "Project slug for docker, lowercase, no spaces" %}
    • PROJECT_NAME - {% translate "Project Name displayed in navbar" %}
    • PROJECT_DESCRIPTION - {% translate "Project Description for footer and SEO" %}
    • PROJECT_DOMAIN - {% translate "Project Domain for URL and emails (use localhost for dev)" %}
    • PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER - {% translate "Port number for local development (default 8000)" %}
    PROJECT_SLUG=myproject
PROJECT_NAME=My Project
PROJECT_DESCRIPTION=A brief description
PROJECT_DOMAIN=localhost
PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER=8000

    {% translate "6. Start the app" %}

    docker compose up -d

    {% translate "7. Run migrations" %}

    docker compose exec django_app python manage.py migrate

    {% translate "8. Create admin user" %}

    docker compose exec django_app python manage.py createsuperuser

    {% translate "9. Open the app" %}

    {% translate "App:" %} http://localhost:8000 | {% translate "Admin:" %} http://localhost:8000/admin

    {% translate "Note: If you changed PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER in .env, use that port instead of 8000." %}

    {% translate "For nerds:" %} Localias {% translate "- real HTTPS domains for local dev" %}

    {% translate "Get real HTTPS domains like" %} myproject.local {% translate "instead of localhost:8000. No need to remember port numbers!" %}

    • {% translate "Test cookies and sessions like in production" %}
    • {% translate "No more CORS issues when calling APIs" %}
    • {% translate "Test HTTPS-only features locally" %}
    • {% translate "Share your local site with others on the same Wi-Fi" %}
    {% translate "1. Install" %}
    brew install peterldowns/tap/localias  # macOS

    {% translate "Other platforms:" %} https://github.com/peterldowns/localias/releases

    {% translate "2. Add alias and start" %}
    localias set myproject.local 8000
localias start

    {% translate "Replace myproject with your PROJECT_SLUG and 8000 with your PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER" %}

    {% translate "3. Open" %}

    {% translate "Visit" %} https://myproject.local

    {% translate "Useful Commands" %}

    {% translate "Command" %} {% translate "Description" %}
    docker compose logs -f {% translate "View logs" %}
    docker compose down {% translate "Stop containers" %}
    docker compose up --build -d {% translate "Rebuild after code changes" %}
    docker compose exec django_app <command> {% translate "Run management commands" %}
    docker compose exec django_app python manage.py migrate {% translate "Run migrations" %}

    {% translate "Manual Setup" %}

    {% translate "Prerequisites" %}

    • Python 3.10+
    • pip
    • Git

    {% translate "1. Create Your Repository" %}

    {% translate "Click" %} {% translate "Use this template" %} {% translate "on" %} {% translate "GitHub" %} {% translate "to create your own repo." %}

    {% translate "2. Clone" %}

    git clone <repository-url> <project-name>
cd <project-name>

    {% translate "3. Create .env" %}

    cp .env.example .env

    {% translate "4. Edit .env" %}

    {% translate "Edit the .env file with your project details. The required fields are:" %}

    • PROJECT_SLUG - {% translate "Project slug for docker, lowercase, no spaces" %}
    • PROJECT_NAME - {% translate "Project Name displayed in navbar" %}
    • PROJECT_DESCRIPTION - {% translate "Project Description for footer and SEO" %}
    • PROJECT_DOMAIN - {% translate "Project Domain for URL and emails (use localhost for dev)" %}
    • PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER - {% translate "Port number for local development (default 8000)" %}
    PROJECT_SLUG=myproject
PROJECT_NAME=My Project
PROJECT_DESCRIPTION=A brief description
PROJECT_DOMAIN=localhost
PROJECT_PORT_NUMBER=8000

    {% translate "5. Create venv and install" %}

    python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Linux/Mac
# venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows
pip install -r requirements/development.txt

    {% translate "6. Migrate" %}

    python manage.py migrate

    {% translate "7. Create admin user" %}

    python manage.py createsuperuser

    {% translate "8. Run and open" %}

    python manage.py runserver

    {% translate "Open" %} http://127.0.0.1:8000 | {% translate "Admin:" %} http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin

    {% translate "Next Steps" %}

    • {% translate "Understand the project structure" %}
    • {% translate "Configure settings" %}
    • {% translate "Explore built-in features" %}
    • {% translate "Deploy to production" %}
    {% endblock %}
